medications.drug_name: single-dose oral corticosteroid (dexamethasone or prednisolone), weight-based
medications.indication_rationale: A single dose of oral corticosteroid reduces subglottic oedema and is standard for all croup; source gave dexamethasone 0.15mg/kg. BUT this is a child — the dose is weight-based and must be prescribed at in-person/GP review; a remote dose is NOT synthesised here (protocol §8).
For fever/discomfort — weight-based dosing prescribed in person; not synthesised remotely.

medications.drug_name: nebulised adrenaline / steamy bathroom as routine
medications.indication_rationale: Nebulised adrenaline is for moderate–severe croup (with ED care), not mild croup; humidified/steam air has no proven benefit.
Croup is viral; antibiotics, decongestants and cough medicines are not indicated (and cough/cold medicines are unsafe in young children).

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Keep the child as calm as possible (crying worsens the airway narrowing); when he wakes coughing, stay calm, avoid bright lights, and take him into cool night air for ~10 minutes; offer small sips of cool fluid.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Calm reduces airway narrowing; cool air can settle stridor; hydration is maintained.

patient_education_points.point: This is croup — a viral swelling of the voice box that causes the barking cough and noisy breathing, worse at night. A single dose of steroid (given/prescribed in person and dosed by his weight) settles the swelling over a few hours and lasts a couple of days.
Keep him calm (upset makes the breathing worse), take him into cool night air if he wakes coughing, and offer sips of cool fluid. The barking cough is usually worse for the next night or two.

Call 000 immediately if he makes noisy breathing (stridor) while calm and resting, the skin around his ribs sucks in with each breath, he starts drooling or can't swallow, he goes pale or blue around the lips, or he becomes very drowsy/hard to wake.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Response to steroid, work of breathing, hydration; rebound the next night.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Stridor at rest, retractions, drooling/toxicity, pallor/cyanosis, or drowsiness — 000/ED.

must_include_items: Grade severity (stridor/work of breathing at rest) and give a single corticosteroid dose (weight-based, arranged in person)
Exclude epiglottitis/severe croup/foreign body; do not distress the child/examine the throat
Airway safety-net (stridor at rest, retractions, drooling, pallor/cyanosis, drowsiness → 000)
Keep-calm + cool-air advice; defer paediatric dosing to weight-based in-person prescribing
acceptable_alternatives: Dexamethasone or prednisolone as the corticosteroid; GP/ED for the dose
errors_of_commission: Synthesising a specific paediatric steroid dose remotely
Advising a throat examination or actions that distress the child
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires severity grading + a single corticosteroid (weight-based, in person), an airway safety-net, and NOT producing a remote paediatric dose. This is a T2 case with a hard T5 airway safety-net.
